item.title: UK ministers gain power to force pension funds to invest in British companies
item.description: Government outlines safeguards to resolve a stand-off with peers over legislation

item.title: Chinese wind turbine maker accuses UK of ‘politicisation’ over product ban
item.description: Ming Yang warns setback will not stop company’s expansion plans in Europe

item.title: CVC pumps cash into Lipton as €4.5bn tea deal comes off the boil
item.description: Private equity firm injects €210mn amid fears of a debt restructuring at Unilever’s former tea business

item.title: EU takes action against Meta over failure to protect children on social media
item.description: Platform could be fined as debate intensifies in Europe over banning minors from sites such as Instagram and Facebook

item.title: Lloyds profits soar 33% as higher interest rates boost income
item.description: Bank slashes expectations for UK growth but keeps guidance steady

item.title: Aston Martin secures £50mn cash injection to shore up balance sheet
item.description: Funding from consortium backed by billionaire Lawrence Stroll comes after fifth consecutive quarterly loss

item.title: Total profits jump 29% as war drives oil price surge and trading gains
item.description: French oil major manages to increase production outside Gulf, offsetting shutdowns caused by the conflict

item.title: UBS trading gains fuel 80% profit surge
item.description: Market volatility from Middle East war propels strong activity at Swiss bank

item.title: A quick taxonomy of fund finance
item.description: Catching up on stuff we should know but maybe didn’t read

item.title: China’s Mao-era regulator in a stand-off with Meta over AI
item.description: National Development Reform Commission is becoming Beijing’s chief enforcer

item.title: FirstFT: Ex-FBI chief James Comey charged with threatening Trump’s life
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: talks over frozen Hungary funds, and emerging market stocks soar

item.title: Introducing: The Story of Money
item.description: A new video podcast from The FT exploring the history of finance

item.title: How Vis Raghavan landed at Citi
item.description: The investment banker was hired by Citi before news of his changed circumstances at JPMorgan was made public

item.title: Ken Griffin suggests wealthy individuals do not understand private credit risks
item.description: Founder of Citadel says some investors may not have realised they cannot quickly withdraw all their money from funds

item.title: UAE opens up an Opec fissure
item.description: Exit represents a rejection of the production-control system dominated in recent years by Saudi Arabia

item.title: Iran war oil price windfall gives Shell a chance to edge ahead of BP
item.description: Extra funds mean majors can now in theory increase exploration without cutting shareholder returns

item.title: Chinese green technology poses national security problem for Europe, report warns
item.description: European countries want to accelerate the shift to solar, wind and other energy sources whose supply chains are dominated by China

item.title: Can Opec survive the UAE’s exit?
item.description: Abu Dhabi’s surprise decision to leave the oil cartel raises doubts over its future

item.title: The great commodities disruption
item.description: Trump’s attack on Iran has served as a reminder of how interconnected the global economy remains

item.title: Memory chipmakers hope AI frenzy signals end to boom and bust
item.description: SK Hynix and Samsung say customers now want long-term contracts to guarantee supplies amid acute shortages

item.title: How can we prevent our children from fighting over our estate?
item.description: We worry they will fall out over their inheritance

item.title: The stablecoin stumbling block
item.description: The crypto tokens are not yet safe enough to be used at scale in the settlement of large, wholesale financial transactions

item.title: KPMG shuts US government audit practice after losing army contract
item.description: Pentagon consolidates work in push to modernise its accounts following years of failed inspections

item.title: X时代的愤怒
item.description: It feels like politics is more fraught today — is it?

item.title: Britain’s top furniture designers are made in Milan
item.description: Tom Dixon’s control of his own brand makes him an outlier among London creatives

item.title: How Citi’s $52mn hire was forced out of JPMorgan over his behaviour
item.description: After years of complaints, Vis Raghavan had been told he was out of a job. Three days later, Citi hired him

item.title: Apple’s next chief John Ternus confronts break from China playbook
item.description: Rising memory costs and Donald Trump’s pressure to invest in US manufacturing will force tough early calls

item.title: How AI is powering the next generation of robotaxis
item.description: Technological advances have propelled self-driving cars from small-scale testing to rapid global expansion

item.title: Hitting the Buffers: The 1873 railway bust that broke one of America’s greatest financiers
item.description: How a truly transformative technology can still cause a financial crash

item.title: The battle of the AI labs
item.description: A handful of Silicon Valley companies want to lead the world in AI. Who will win?
